'''Tony Gigot''' (born 27 December 1990) is a French former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|fb|so|ce}} or {{rlp|sh}} for [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level and [[Racing Club Albi XIII|Albi XIII]] in the [[Elite One Championship]] and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[London Broncos]], [[Sporting Olympique Avignon]], [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in two separate spells.

==Background==
Gigot was born in [[Avignon]], France. His younger brother [[Samuel Gigot|Samuel]] is a professional footballer.

==Club career==
===Early career===
[[File:Tony Gigot.JPG|thumb|right|Gigot playing for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in 2010]]
Gigot spent time in the youth team at the [[London Broncos]]. He signed with the Catalans Dragons in 2010 and made his [[Super League]] debut that year, playing a total of 15 games and kicking two goals. He also represented Catalans in the 2010 Challenge Cup where he played 2 games and scored 1 try.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}
[[File:Tony Gigot 10.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Gigot playing for France in 2010]]
In 2011, Gigot played 7 games throughout the season for the Catalans Dragons, kicking 1 goal. He once again represented Catalans in the 2011 Challenge Cup, where he played 1 game.

As well as playing for the Catalans Dragons, in 2011 Gigot played 9 games on loan for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Co-operative Championship|2011 Championship]]. He scored 5 tries.

After being released by Catalans, Gigot signed with the [[Avignon Bisons]] to play in the 2012-2013 [[Elite One Championship]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks===
Gigot was given an early release by Avignon at the start of 2013 so he could travel to Australia for a chance to play in the Australian [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) competition. Gigot spent pre-season training with [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] before signing a one-year deal with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]]. He did not feature for the Sharks at NRL level, however.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===London Broncos===
In February 2014, [[Super League]] club [[London Broncos]] announced the signing of Tony Gigot for the 2014 season. He played two matches and kicked four goals before leaving the club to re-sign with Avignon.{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

===Return to Catalans===
In May 2015, the [[Catalans Dragons]] announced the resigning of Gigot. He made his second debut for the club playing as a centre in a 37–34 victory over [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[2015 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]]. The following week he made his Super League return for the Dragons, scoring a hat-trick in a 58–14 victory over [[Wigan Warriors]].{{citation needed|date=June 2020}}

On 10 February 2017 it was announced that Gigot had been banned for two years by the French rugby league authorities for an incident with an anti-doping official during a training camp in October 2016.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.francebleu.fr/sports/rugby/dragons-catalans-tony-gigot-suspendu-1486735296|title=Dragons catalans : deux ans de suspension pour Tony Gigot|date=10 February 2017|access-date=11 February 2017}}</ref> It was reported that Gigot had not failed a drug test and that supported by his club, Gigot appealed the ban.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/10762354/catalans-dragons-full-back-tony-gigot-banned-over-clash-with-doping-official|title=Catalans Dragons full-back Tony Gigot banned over clash with doping official
|date=10 February 2017|access-date=11 February 2017}}</ref> The appeal against the length of the ban was successful and with the ban reduced to three months, Gigot was able to play again from April 2017.<ref>{{cite news |title=Gigot makes winning return |newspaper=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |issue=3065 |date=10 April 2017 |page=8}}</ref> However, in August 2017 the full two-year ban was reinstated by the French anti-doping agency for the "inappropriate exchange" with the anti-doping official.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/10972618/catalans-dragons-tony-gigot-receives-two-year-ban |title=Catalans Dragons' Tony Gigot receives two-year ban |publisher=[[Sky Sports]] |date=4 August 2017 |access-date=10 February 2018}}</ref> Gigot and the club launched a further appeal which was successful and on 9 February 2018 it was announced that Gigot was free to resume playing.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/news/gigot-wins-appeal-against-ban/142739 |title=Catalans Dragons' Tony Gigot wins appeal against ban |publisher=Sporting Life |date=9 February 2018 |access-date=10 February 2018}}</ref>

On 5 August 2018 Gigot was named Man of the Match in Catalans' 35–16 victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the semi-final of the [[Challenge Cup]]. At the end of the final on 25 August he was named as the winner of the [[Lance Todd Trophy]], becoming the first French player to win the trophy as Catalans beat [[Warrington Wolves]] 20–14.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.thetimes.com/sport/rugby-league/article/tony-gigot-fires-catalans-dragons-to-first-challenge-cup-hmw83l2cl |title=Tony Gigot fires Catalans Dragons to first Challenge Cup |author=Chris Irvine |newspaper=[[The Times]] |date=25 August 2018 |access-date=25 August 2018}}</ref>

===Toronto Wolfpack===
At the end of 2019, Gigot and Catalans Dragons failed to come to terms on a new contract. As a result, Gigot started the 2020 Super League season without a team and trained with Avignon to stay fit. In February 2020, the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] announced that Gigot would join the team for a four-week, unpaid trial period.<ref>{{cite news 
|title = Tony Gigot: Toronto Wolfpack sign ex-Catalans Dragons half-back on four-week trial
|publisher = BBC
|date = 2020-02-19
|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/51565425
|access-date = 2020-03-03}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
Gigot signed with [[Wakefield Trinity]] on 23 July 2020 on a deal until the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite news |title=Tony Gigot: Wakefield sign former Catalans back until end of 2021 season |publisher=BBC Sport |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/53517175 |access-date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Craven |first1=Dave |title=Wakefield Trinity's Chris Chester "over the moon" at Tony Gigot signing |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinitys-chris-chester-over-moon-tony-gigot-signing-2922634 |website=Wakefield Express |access-date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Trinity secure the signing of Tony Gigot |url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/blog/2020/07/23/trinity-secure-the-signing-of-tony-gigot/ |website=Wakefield Trinity |date=23 July 2020 |publisher=Wakefield Trinity |access-date=29 September 2020}}</ref> On 3 January 2021 Wakefield and Gigot cancelled his contract by mutual consent a due to homesickness.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=BBC Sport|date=3 January 2021|access-date=3 January 2021|title=Tony Gigot: Wakefield Trinity back leaves due to homesickness|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55522353}}</ref>

===Sporting Olympique Avignon (re-join)===
On 29 January 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Sporting Olympique Avignon]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 January 2021|access-date=30 January 2021|title=Tony Gigot returns to France with Avignon|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tony-gigot-returns-to-france-with-avignon/}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 7 May 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 May 2021|accessdate=8 May 2021|title=Tony Gigot joins Toulouse|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tony-gigot-joins-toulouse/}}</ref> In January 2022, Gigot was appointed captain of Toulouse,<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.to13.com/a-la-une/super-league-j-15-les-capitaine-et-vice-capitaine-annonces/ | title=Super League : J – 15, les Capitaine et Vice-Capitaine annoncés | date=28 January 2022 }}</ref> following the sudden departure of [[Johnathon Ford]].

==International career==
Gigot made his first appearance for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in the [[2010 European Cup]], scoring one try. Tony played for France in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]], [[2014 European Cup]] and [[2015 European Cup]], a tournament where Tony topped the try scoring charts with 4 tries in the 3 games France played. He played in the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|2016 end of year test match]] against England in Avignon, kicking a goal in France's 6–40 loss. Gigot represented France at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] and played in all three group stage matches as France recorded one victory and two losses.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-10-31/samoa-france-tonga-rugby-league-world-cup/101595580/|title=Samoa thumps France at Rugby League World Cup to set up quarterfinal clash with Tonga|publisher=Australian Broadcasting Corporation}}</ref>
